name: Setaka Toko

appearance.hair: Turquoise, spiky, voluminous. Does not match the rest of his presentation but he likes it.
appearance.build: Enormous. Heavily muscular. The tallest person in the class by several inches.
appearance.other: Iron cannon barrels protrude from his knuckles — two per hand, thick and heavy. Always present. He learned to type, open doors, and do most things as a child with them. His handshake is famous.

personality.summary: Respectful and honourable. He means what he says, does what he says he'll do, and treats everyone with equal genuine courtesy — classmates, teachers, opponents in a training exercise, the cafeteria staff. This is not a performance or a strategy. He was raised this way and considers it the obvious way to be.
personality.in_class: Polite, punctual, prepared. Helps people without being asked when he notices they could use it.
personality.in_combat: Formal in a way that's slightly unusual for combat — announces his attack before it lands if there's time, acknowledges good moves from opponents. People find this unsettling.
personality.quirk_of_character: He writes thank-you notes. Physical ones. He carries cards. Other students have received these after normal interactions that Setaka found meaningful.

ability_scores.note: STR primary — Iron Ball's cannon fire and close-range strikes are raw physical projection. CON secondary for sustained combat durability at his size.

class_note: Heroic Vanguard → Unarmed Style. His cannon barrels are natural weapons and his kit suits sustained melee with ranged burst capacity.

quirk_mechanics.weakness: Launching larger balls strains his muscles and stamina. If he launches three Large balls or one Massive ball in a single encounter, he must succeed on a DC 16 CON save or gain a level of exhaustion — and on a failure, Large and Massive balls are disabled for 3 rounds.

abilities.name: Iron Ball
abilities.type: Action; switch ball size as a bonus action or reaction
abilities.description: Launch an iron ball from his knuckles, choosing the size as part of the attack: Small/Pellet (2d10 bludgeoning, 80 ft range, pierces armor by ignoring half cover, can ricochet to hit a second target within 10 ft for 1d10); Medium/Standard (4d10 bludgeoning, 40 ft range, 10 ft knockback on hit); Large/Crushing Ball (6d10 bludgeoning, 20 ft range or melee, double damage to structures, creates a 15-ft radius of difficult terrain, 15 ft knockback on hit); or Massive/Overload Shot (10d10 bludgeoning, 10 ft radius hitting all creatures in range, STR save vs Quirk DC or knocked prone, triple damage to structures, creates a 25-ft radius of difficult terrain). Smaller balls trade damage for range; larger balls trade range for damage and battlefield control.

dm_notes.combat_role: Durable melee fighter with switchable ranged burst capability. He's the class's tank with the ability to trade range for damage and battlefield control by stepping up his ball size. Massive/Overload Shot is his set-piece move — save it for moments where knocking everything nearby prone at once will turn a fight.
dm_notes.social_role: Moral north star. When the class is considering doing something questionable, Setaka doesn't lecture — he just says what he would do, clearly, and moves on. The contrast is felt.
dm_notes.growth_arc: His honour code will be genuinely tested. A situation where following it perfectly causes real harm, or where breaking it slightly could prevent something bad. He will make a decision. He will live with it.
dm_notes.weakness_to_exploit: Repeated use of Large or Massive balls risks a CON save failure that costs him a level of exhaustion and locks out his heaviest options for several rounds. DEX is average — he's not particularly mobile. Forcing him to keep escalating ball size in a long fight is the way to break him.
